team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$5M in space before restructures, but significant flexibility available via restructures of Moore (traded), Thuney, Jarrett contracts. Released Edmunds, traded Moore, Dalman retired — created ~$44M in space this offseason. Still navigating expensive deals for Jarrett and Odeyingbo.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers at DL, EDGE, and S. Willing to move overpaid veterans (Jarrett, Kmet, Odeyingbo) if return is right. Just traded DJ Moore for a 2nd-round pick. Team is building around Caleb Williams' rookie deal window — next 2 years are critical.

players.name: Caleb Williams
players.performance_evidence: 3,942 passing yards (franchise record), 27 TDs, 7 INTs in 2025. Led Bears to 11-6, NFC North title, first playoff win since 2010. PFF overall grade of 75.4 (16th among QBs). Set franchise passing record. 6.94 Y/A — still developing but already a top-15 QB with elite upside on a rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old franchise QB on a rookie deal with 2 years remaining plus 5th-year option. Led team to division title in Year 2. One of the most valuable trade assets in the NFL — no realistic pick package would satisfy the Bears.

players.availability_reasoning: Face of the franchise, #1 overall pick, just led the Bears to their first division title since 2018 on a team-friendly rookie deal. Zero chance of a trade under any circumstances.

players.name: Tyson Bagent
players.performance_evidence: Fan-favorite UDFA backup who showed flashes in spot starts during 2023. Signed a 2-year extension through 2027. Minimal playing time behind Williams in 2025. Capable backup with limited upside.

trade_value.reasoning: Young backup QB on a cheap deal with some starting experience. Worth a late-round pick to a QB-needy team, but the Bears value his presence in the QB room.

players.availability_reasoning: Bears re-signed Case Keenum as a veteran presence. Bagent could be available if a team offers a mid-round pick, but Bears value having a capable young backup behind Williams.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN hinted Bears could trade Bagent after re-signing Keenum.

players.name: D'Andre Swift
players.performance_evidence: 1,087 rushing yards (12th in NFL), career-high 9 rushing TDs in 2025. Scored the first TD in the Bears' wild card comeback vs. Packers. Improved significantly under Ben Johnson after a rough 2024 (3.8 YPC). Shared carries with Monangai down the stretch.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring contract with no guaranteed money remaining caps his value. Solid production but RB market is depressed. Worth a 4th-round pick at best. The Bears may let him play out the deal.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal, no guarantees remaining, and 7th-round rookie Kyle Monangai is emerging as a capable RB2 who could take over. Bears would listen to offers but won't give him away.
players.injury_notes: Groin injury caused him to miss Week 9 in 2025. History of soft tissue injuries throughout career.

players.name: Kyle Monangai
players.performance_evidence: 7th-round steal who earned significant playing time as a rookie. Shared 100-yard rushing games with Swift in Week 13 vs. Eagles. Physical runner from Rutgers who rushed for 1,279 yards in college. Growing role in Ben Johnson's offense.

trade_value.reasoning: Promising young RB on a minimum-level deal with 3 years of control. Worth a 6th-round pick but the Bears view him as Swift's potential successor and won't move him cheaply.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap, ascending talent who could be the future RB1. Bears have no reason to trade him unless overwhelmed with an offer.

players.name: Rome Odunze
players.performance_evidence: Part of the Bears' balanced passing attack in 2025 — targets were distributed evenly among Loveland, Moore, Odunze, and Burden. Expected to become WR1 with DJ Moore traded. 2nd-year player with high ceiling — 6'3, 4.45 speed, consensus All-American at Washington. 5th-year option available for 2028.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old former top-10 pick on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years + 5th-year option remaining. Now the WR1 after Moore's departure. Young WR1s on rookie deals are among the most valuable trade assets in football.

players.availability_reasoning: Just became the de facto WR1 after the DJ Moore trade. On a team-friendly rookie deal with years of control. The Bears built this offense around Williams and his young weapons — Odunze is a cornerstone.
players.injury_notes: Was questionable for the playoff game vs. Rams in 2025 but played.

players.name: Luther Burden III
players.performance_evidence: Impressive rookie season — produced as part of Chicago's balanced attack. Connected with Williams on a 65-yard TD in Week 3. Received the nickname 'Iceman' co-creator for Williams. Former 5-star recruit with elite burst and acceleration. One of four Bears pass-catchers separated by just 60 receiving yards on the season.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old ascending WR on a fully guaranteed rookie deal with 3 years of control. Former high second-round pick with first-round talent who showed immediate impact. Elite upside on a cheap contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Just drafted him 10 months ago to be a core part of the Williams-era offense. With Moore traded, Burden's role only grows. Zero chance the Bears move him.
players.injury_notes: Suffered soft tissue injury during rookie minicamp but was ready for training camp.

players.name: Jahdae Walker
players.performance_evidence: Depth WR who emerged as the WR3 after DJ Moore was traded mid-season. Listed on depth chart but minimal individual production data available. Slot/depth option in Ben Johnson's system.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth WR with no significant trade value. Essentially a replacement-level player on a minimum deal.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece who could be moved easily. Minimal investment or attachment.

players.name: Colston Loveland
players.performance_evidence: Led the Bears in receiving as a rookie — the top target in Williams' passing attack. Drafted 10th overall as the first TE off the board. Compared to Sam LaPorta. 6'6 with elite route-running and catch radius. Immediately became the centerpiece of Ben Johnson's passing game.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old rookie TE1 who led the team in receiving on a team-friendly rookie deal with 3 years + 5th-year option. Highest-drafted Bears TE since Mike Ditka. Essentially untradeable — a franchise building block.

players.availability_reasoning: Rookie first-round pick who immediately became the #1 target. Face of the offense alongside Williams. The Bears specifically drafted him to be the centerpiece of the passing game for the next decade.
players.injury_notes: Had AC joint surgery in January 2025 (pre-draft) and a hip injury during the 2025 season but played through both.

players.name: Cole Kmet
players.performance_evidence: 30 catches, 347 yards, 2 TDs in 2025 — worst season since rookie year. Overshadowed by Loveland. Made two clutch playoff TDs (vs. Eagles, vs. Rams) including the incredible 4th-down fadeaway catch from Williams. Good blocker, respected leader, but production has cratered with Loveland's emergence.

trade_value.reasoning: Still a capable starting TE who can block and make clutch catches, but overpaid at $11.6M cap hit for a TE2 role. Trading him before March 15 saves $8.4M. Worth a 4th-round pick to a TE-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac/OTC confirm no guaranteed money remaining. Trading him before March 15 saves $8.4M in cap space. Loveland has taken over as TE1. Beat reporters have noted Kmet as a potential cap casualty. Multiple reports indicate the Bears are considering moving on.
players.character_notes: Beloved teammate, locker room leader, local Chicago-area product. Makes a trade emotionally harder but financially logical.
players.trade_buzz: Multiple reports from Spotrac, Windy City Gridiron, and beat reporters indicate Kmet is a trade/cut candidate this offseason to create cap space.

players.name: Braxton Jones
players.performance_evidence: Started 44 career games at LT. Had a 77.4 PFF grade in 2024 (top 20 among OTs) but was benched early in 2025 after struggling upon return from ankle injury. Re-signed on a 1-year 'prove it' deal as a bridge while Ozzy Trapilo recovers from torn patellar tendon.

trade_value.reasoning: Bridge starter on a 1-year deal. Below-average LT who provides roster stability but no long-term upside. Worth a 5th-round pick at most. Dead cap nearly equals cap hit, limiting trade savings.

players.availability_reasoning: One-year deal signed specifically as a placeholder while Trapilo recovers. If the Bears draft an OT or find a better option, Jones could become expendable. But dead cap limits trade benefit.
players.injury_notes: Broke ankle late in 2024. Struggled upon return in 2025, eventually benched. Also spent time on IR with knee injury in 2025.

players.name: Joe Thuney
players.performance_evidence: 4-time Super Bowl champion, 3x Pro Bowler, 2x First-Team All-Pro. Was First-Team All-Pro and finished 10th in OPOY voting in 2024 with the Chiefs before being traded to the Bears. Anchored the Bears' 3rd-ranked rushing offense in 2025. Still elite at age 32.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite guard production but age (33) and massive dead cap ($26M) make a trade essentially impossible. His 2026 salary is fully guaranteed. Worth a 2nd-round pick on talent alone but the contract structure makes him untradeable.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap ($26M) exceeds his cap hit ($21.5M). Trading him would cost the Bears more than keeping him. Fully guaranteed salary in 2026. The Bears paid a premium to acquire and extend him to protect Caleb Williams — he's not going anywhere.
players.character_notes: Consummate professional. Reunited with college teammate Garrett Bradbury in Chicago.

players.name: Garrett Bradbury
players.performance_evidence: Started all 17 regular season games and all 4 playoff games for the Patriots in 2025, including Super Bowl LX. 105 career starts. Solid, reliable center who fits Ben Johnson's zone-blocking scheme. Not spectacular but consistently available.

trade_value.reasoning: 31-year-old center on an expiring 1-year deal. Competent starter but limited upside and age. Worth a late 5th at most. The Bears just traded for him specifically to replace Drew Dalman.

players.availability_reasoning: One-year rental acquired to replace retired Drew Dalman. If the Bears draft a center, Bradbury could become expendable. But he's cheap enough that they're likely to keep him.
players.injury_notes: Durable — hasn't missed a start in 2 years.

players.name: Jonah Jackson
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl in 2021 with Detroit. Traded from Rams to Bears in 2025 and given a 1-year extension. Had a solid 2025 season helping the Bears' rushing attack rank 3rd in the NFL. However, $25M cap hit in 2026 is the most expensive for any guard in the NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: Good starter but massively overpaid at $25M cap hit. Acquiring team would need to restructure. If Bears cut pre-March 16 they save $18M eating $7M dead cap. Worth a 3rd-4th round pick if contract is restructured.

players.availability_reasoning: The $25M cap hit makes him a restructure-or-cut candidate. Bears could save $18M by releasing him before March 16. If they keep him, they'll likely convert salary to bonus to lower the cap hit. Available if a team will take the contract.
players.injury_notes: Injury history — has been consistently hurt or questionable over the past 3-4 years per reporting.

players.name: Darnell Wright
players.performance_evidence: Second-Team All-Pro in 2025. PFF's 7th-best run-blocking grade (85.6) among OTs. Driving force behind Bears' 3rd-ranked rushing offense (144.5 YPG). Allowed only 3 sacks and 19 pressures. Played most of the year with a torn UCL in his elbow. 5th-year option ($20.5M) expected to be exercised; long-term extension projected at 4yr/$92M+.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old All-Pro RT on a rookie deal with 5th-year option. One of the most valuable non-QB trade assets in football. Teams would offer multiple first-round picks. The Bears will extend him — he's a cornerstone of their offensive line.

players.availability_reasoning: Young All-Pro tackle on a cheap deal protecting the franchise QB. The Bears are expected to exercise his 5th-year option and sign him to a massive extension. Trading him would be franchise malpractice.
players.injury_notes: Played most of 2025 with a torn UCL in his right elbow, missing only the Week 4 game. Toughness is a plus.

players.name: Montez Sweat
players.performance_evidence: 10 sacks, 50+ tackles, 3 forced fumbles in 2025. Has been the Bears' best defensive player since his acquisition. 35 sacks over the past 3 seasons in Chicago. Consistent double-digit sack threat. Spotrac projects a 3yr/$66M extension.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber EDGE with 2 years remaining at $24.5M AAV. Age 30 limits long-term value, but elite production and reasonable dead cap ($8.2M) make him tradeable if the Bears pivoted to a rebuild. Worth a late 1st to a contender.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive piece and the Bears' best pass rusher. They're expected to extend him. Only available if a team offers a massive overpay (1st-round pick+). The Bears are competing — they won't weaken their defense voluntarily.
players.character_notes: Team leader, community pillar in Chicago.
players.trade_buzz: No trade buzz. Extension talks expected this offseason per Spotrac projections.

players.name: Grady Jarrett
players.performance_evidence: PFF overall grade of 52.8 in 2025 (94th among 129 DL). Pass rush grade of 43.5 (106th). Massive regression from his Pro Bowl days in Atlanta. Only 1.5 sacks in 2025. The $43.5M contract is widely viewed as a major overpay by Poles.

trade_value.reasoning: Essentially untradeable due to dead cap ($21M) exceeding cap hit ($19M). No team will take on this contract for a 33-year-old declining DT. The Bears are stuck with him through 2026. Negative trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: The Bears would love to move on, but the dead cap ($21M) makes a pre-June 1 trade/cut a net negative. A post-June 1 move saves $15M with $4M dead cap, which is the most likely escape route. No realistic trade partner will absorb this contract.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in Week 8 of 2023. Returned in 2024 but performance has declined steadily.
players.trade_buzz: Multiple reports from DaWindyCity and beat reporters describe his contract as a 'regrettable' signing. Bears are exploring creative ways to mitigate the cap damage.

players.name: Gervon Dexter Sr.
players.performance_evidence: Career year in 2025: 6 sacks, 11 QB hits, 39 total pressures. PFF pass-rushing grade of 78.9 (7th among DTs). Started all 17 games. Has improved every season since being drafted. Only 24 years old and entering final year of rookie deal — extension candidate.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old ascending DT on a dirt-cheap final year of his rookie deal ($2.1M cap hit). Top-10 pass-rushing DT. Thin DT FA and draft markets make him even more valuable. Worth a 2nd-round pick but the Bears desperately want to keep him.

players.availability_reasoning: Bears insider Brad Biggs shut down trade rumors: 'They're not taking calls on him, they're not making calls on him.' However, he's entering the final year of his deal and Jarrett's contract makes an extension financially difficult. If a team offers a 1st, the Bears would have to think hard.
players.character_notes: NIL lawsuit from college (Gervon sued over predatory NIL deal) — no on-field impact.
players.trade_buzz: Jordan Schultz reported teams have inquired about trading for Dexter. Brad Biggs shut it down, saying Bears aren't listening. DT market thinness makes him highly coveted.

players.name: Dayo Odeyingbo
players.performance_evidence: Played only 8 games in 2025 before season-ending Achilles tear. 21 tackles, 4 QB hits, 1 sack in limited action. Signed a 3yr/$48M deal but has been a major disappointment — just 9 pressures in 183 snaps before injury. PFF ranked him 85th among EDGE rushers in pressures.

trade_value.reasoning: Negative trade value due to massive guaranteed money ($15.5M) and Achilles injury. No team will absorb this contract for a player coming off a devastating injury with poor production. The Bears are exploring injury settlement options.

players.availability_reasoning: Bears would move him in a heartbeat but the contract is toxic. $15.5M in dead cap makes a straight trade painful. Injury settlement has been floated as an option but is a long shot given the guarantees involved. Likely stuck on the roster.
players.injury_notes: Suffered season-ending Achilles tear in November 2025. Previously tore his Achilles before the 2021 draft. Two Achilles tears on the same player is a major red flag for recovery.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters and fan sites have extensively discussed ways to get out of his contract. Injury settlement has been floated but appears unlikely given the guaranteed money.

players.name: Devin Bush
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 3yr/$30M deal with the Bears in 2026 free agency. Former 10th overall pick who had a career resurgence with the Steelers and then as a free agent. Adds athleticism and speed to the LB group following Tremaine Edmunds' release. Stabilizes the linebacker corps per SI reporting.

trade_value.reasoning: Newly signed FA on a 3-year deal. Athletic LB who fills a need. Worth a mid-4th but the Bears just signed him — they're not trading him immediately. Dead cap is prohibitive in year 1.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a free agent to replace Edmunds. The Bears invested in him specifically to fill their LB need. Not available in the short term.
players.injury_notes: History of ACL tear in Pittsburgh (2020) that derailed his early career. Has been healthy recently.

players.name: Noah Sewell
players.performance_evidence: 3rd-round pick who has been a rotational piece. Was placed on injured reserve during the 2025 season. Young and athletic but hasn't fully broken out as a starter. Playing behind Devin Bush on the depth chart.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap LB with 2 years of control. Athletic but hasn't established himself as a starter. Worth a 6th-round pick. Low cost, low return.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth LB on a cheap deal. Bears would listen to offers but there's no urgency to move him. Could develop into a larger role.
players.injury_notes: Placed on injured reserve during 2025 season.

players.name: Jaylon Johnson
players.performance_evidence: 2023 Second-Team All-Pro, Pro Bowl. Led NFL in passer rating allowed when targeted (33.3) in 2023. Allowed just 25 catches for 195 yards per PFF in breakout 2023 season. Missed significant time in 2025 on IR (placed on IR in October, returned for playoffs). When healthy, he's a top-10 corner.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl CB on a fair deal ($19M AAV) with 2 years remaining. When healthy, top-10 at the position. But 2025 was marred by injuries — he missed significant time. Age 27 is still prime. Worth a high 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive piece and the best CB on the roster. Bears invested in him long-term. Only available for a clear overpay (1st-round pick). Beat reporters have noted the Bears might let him walk after 2027 if they can't afford to keep the secondary together.
players.injury_notes: Placed on IR in October 2025 (groin). Returned for the playoffs. Also missed time in 2024 with injuries. Durability is a growing concern.

players.name: Tyrique Stevenson
players.performance_evidence: Started 17 and 14 games in his first two seasons respectively. Extension-eligible after the 2025 season. Reports suggest the Bears may have been phasing him out at times in 2025 but injuries forced his continued playing time. Inconsistent but athletic young corner entering contract year.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old starting CB2 on a final-year rookie deal at just $2.1M. Cheap starter with upside. Worth a mid-4th to a team willing to gamble on a contract-year breakout. Minimal dead cap makes him easy to move.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of his rookie deal with no guarantees. Reports suggest the Bears considered phasing him out. They could save $1.8M by cutting him or get draft capital via trade. With Zah Frazier drafted and Cam Lewis signed, the Bears have CB depth.
players.character_notes: Was involved in a controversy in 2024 when he was seen celebrating before a game-deciding Hail Mary was attempted by Washington, which the Commanders completed for the win.
players.trade_buzz: Some buzz about Bears potentially being willing to move on. Cutting him saves ~$3.5M per Windy City Gridiron.

players.name: Kyler Gordon
players.performance_evidence: Highest-paid slot CB in the NFL ($13.33M/yr). When healthy, one of the best nickel corners in the game. But played only 3 regular season games in 2025 due to two separate IR stints (groin, calf). 5 career INTs, 17 PDs. PFF's 2nd-highest-graded Bears defender in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old elite slot CB signed through 2028. When healthy, top-3 at his position. But 2025 was lost to injuries and the dead cap ($18M) makes a trade very costly. Worth a 2nd-round pick on talent but durability and dead cap are concerns.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed to a long-term extension just last April. Dead cap is massive ($18M) making a trade nearly impossible in 2026. The Bears committed to him as a core piece of their defense. Only available if they get a massive return and can absorb the dead cap hit.
players.injury_notes: Played only 3 regular season games in 2025 due to groin and calf injuries. Two separate IR stints. Also had hand fracture in 2023. Durability is a serious concern.

players.name: Cam Lewis
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 2-year, $10M deal with the Bears in 2026 free agency from Buffalo. Described as a 'versatile piece' who played corner, safety, and nickel for the Bills. Depth/versatility signing who slots in as the starting strong safety.

trade_value.reasoning: Versatile defensive back on a modest 2-year deal. Solid depth signing but no star-level production. Worth a late-round pick at most. Just signed — not being moved.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a free agent to address the safety position. Bears invested in him specifically. Not available short-term.

players.name: Coby Bryant
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 3-year, $40M deal with the Bears in 2026 free agency from Seattle. Super Bowl champion with the Seahawks (Super Bowl LX). Praised for man coverage ability and physicality. Starting free safety in Dennis Allen's defense.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old starting safety on a new 3-year deal. Quality starter with Super Bowl experience. Worth a 3rd-round pick but just signed — dead cap ($18M) makes a trade impossible in year 1.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a major free agency addition. The Bears specifically targeted him to fill their biggest defensive need. Dead cap makes him immovable in 2026.

players.name: T.J. Edwards
players.performance_evidence: Reliable tackling machine who has posted 100+ tackles in multiple seasons. Recorded 130+ combined tackles in 2024, solid run defender and capable in zone coverage. PFF grades consistently in the 65-72 range. Not a flashy playmaker but a steady, high-volume contributor in Chicago's defense. Named a team captain.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting off-ball LB on a reasonable deal with 2 years left, but turning 29 and LB is a lower-value position. Worth roughly a late 3rd/early 4th. UDFA pedigree but has outplayed it significantly.

players.availability_reasoning: Edwards is a solid veteran but not a cornerstone piece. At 29, Chicago could look to get younger at LB if a fair offer came in. The dead cap hit is manageable at $4M. If the Bears view themselves as still building, Edwards is the type of veteran who could net draft capital from a contender.

players.name: D'Marco Jackson
players.performance_evidence: Special teams contributor who has worked his way into a rotational defensive role with the Bears. Limited snap counts on defense — primarily a run-down linebacker who fills in on early downs. Minimal impact as a pass rusher or in coverage. Has flashed decent instincts against the run but hasn't produced consistent stat lines. A 5th-round pick out of Appalachian State who has hung around as a depth/ST piece.

trade_value.reasoning: Rotational LB on a minimum expiring deal with limited production. Essentially replacement-level trade value — a 7th-round pick at best, and most teams would rather just claim a similar player off waivers.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of a cheap deal with no guaranteed money and zero dead cap. Purely a depth piece that Chicago could easily replace through the draft or free agency. Would be moved for any pick offered.
